steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says “genetic research only recently _____ its ancestor to be teosinte grass.” The blank needs a verb meaning ‘showed’ or ‘made known’ that fits the structure ‘[verb] its ancestor to be [noun].’

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: does not collocate naturally with ‘its ancestor to be’ in this construction. ✗
  • Choice B — revealed: ‘revealed its ancestor to be teosinte grass’ is grammatically sound and means the research made this fact known — consistent with ‘only recently.’ ✓
  • Choice C: conflicts grammatically or semantically with ‘its ancestor to be teosinte grass.’ ✗
  • Choice D: conflicts with the idea of a new, recent finding. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
